Subject: USE OF FACE MASKS/SHIELDS IN THE INHALATION FACILITY, WAREHOUSE, QC CHEMISTRY AND QC MICROBIOLOGY LABORATORIES AT AMNEAL IRELAND
Purpose: Provide instruction for the proper use and disposal of face masks/shields in Inhalation Facility, Warehouse, QC Chemistry and QC Microbiology Laboratories at Amneal Ireland in line with the Irish Governments and CDC recommendations.
Scope: Use and disposal of face masks/shields in Inhalation Facility, Warehouse, QC Chemistry and QC Microbiology Laboratories by Amneal Personnel, Contractors and Visitors.
General office areas, Maintenance areas such as the Interstitial area and Pumphouses at Amneal Ireland are out of scope of this SOP.

Definitions.QC/ARD: Quality Control Analytical Research and Development

Procedure: General Instruction for the proper use of Face Masks/Face Shields in conjunction with additional controls
Instructions on wearing and removing masks/ face shields
References: ES-001 Material Destruction and Waste Management Procedure
MF-009 Gowning Procedure for Entering and Exiting the Inhalation Areas
MM-003 Garbing Procedure for Warehouse Entry
QC-029 Gowning Procedure for QC/ ARD Laboratory Entry
QC-073 Gowning Procedure for the Microbiology Laboratory
FDA Good Manufacturing Practice Considerations for Responding to COVID-19 Infection in Employees in Drug and Biological Products Manufacturing June 2020

History.Reason: Section 5.1 updated to include existing control measures Section 5.2.3 updated to include clarity of requirements in CNC office area (XP-15). Numbering of points realigned Section 5.3.3 updated to include clarity of requirements in Warehouse and laboratories while at designated workstations. Numbering of points realigned.

title: Usage of Personal Protective Equipment(PPE)
purpose: To establish a procedure for managing the use of personal protective equipment (PPE) including eye, hand, foot and body protection and to fulfil the requirements of the Safety Health and Welfare at Work (General Applications) Regulations 2007, Part 2, Chapter 3: Use of Personal Protective Equipment at Work.
scope: Applies to all PPE selected, used and maintained on site and to all areas on site where PPE is required

EHS Department: Develop and maintain a PPE matrix for all PPE used on site
Provide technical review and guidance to all users of PPE on site
Assist in hazard identification and risk assessment of the tasks and activities
Provide technical advice on the selection, inspection, use, testing, maintenance and storage of PPE
Develop and support training on PPE requirements

All Employees: Ensure they understand the hazards and PPE requirements related to their work activities and location
Make use of the appropriate PPE as per site rules and risk assessments
Wear, maintain and report lost, damage or concerns of relating to their PPE to EHS

Area Managers: Provide resources necessary to implement and maintain an effective program
Assist in identifying feasible engineering and work practice controls before identifying specific PPE as an primary control measure
Ensure personnel have been trained in proper use and maintenance of PPE
Ensure PPE is utilized for the specific work activities
Ensure that all visitors are issued with the correct PPE as appropriate and that they are made aware of the areas where mandatory PPE is required before entering these areas

definitions.Amneal Ireland: Amneal Ireland Limited Cahir Road, Cashel, Co. Tipperary, Ireland
definitions.Control or Control measures: A process, policy, device, practice or action designed to eliminate or minimize a risk

procedure: Complete and document hazard identification and risk assessment as per ES-009 Risk Assessment Procedure to determine where PPE controls are necessary using the hierarchy of control: Elimination, Substitution, Engineering Controls, Administrative Controls, Personal Protective Equipment.
references: Environmental Health and Safety Risk Assessment Procedure

Purpose: Ensure protection of health, safety and security
Minimization of damage to property and environment
Efficient management of incident reporting and investigating
Scope: Emergency Response Team, employees and contractors.

EmergencyResponseTeam: Providing response in case of major spill.
Considering specific plans for dealing with spills.
Employees: Adherence to processes and procedures for preventing spills.
FacilitiesTeam: Shut off valves or lock pumps in spill response situation.
MicrobiologicalDepartment: Control and clean biological spills in microbiology lab.
ExternalEmergencyServices: Control and clean major chemical spills.

Definitions.AmnealIreland: Amneal Ireland Limited, Cahir Road, Cashel, Co. Tipperary, Ireland.
Definitions.EmergencyResponse: Immediate actions taken by teams or groups which begin the process and procedure to mitigate impact on site or business unit operation.
Definitions.EmergencyResponseTeam: A site-level team of specialized individuals who are activated to provide immediate on-site response.
Definitions.EnvironmentalEvent: Any spill or release, any non-compliance with a permit condition or regulation, any environmental incident which has onsite or the potential to cause off-site impact, any violation of a consent order, any enforcement action from an environmental regulatory authority.
Definitions.HFA: Hydrofluoroalkane, Propellant used for meter dosed inhalers.
Definitions.Rescue: Activities directed at locating endangered persons at an emergency incident, removing those persons from danger, treating the injured, and providing for transport to an appropriate health care facility.
Definitions.Response: Activities that occur in preparation for an emergency and continue until the arrival of emergency apparatus at the scene.

Procedure: Response to a small hazardous liquid spill.
Response to a small hazardous solids spill.
Response to a large hazardous liquid spill or a large hazardous solids spill.
Investigation into the cause of Spill.
